'''''AA-23''''' was a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] in the service of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==History==
''AA-23'' was stationed on an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[XQ6 Platform]] [[DX-11a]] in the [[Eidoloni system/Legends|Eidoloni system]] some time after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. It was one of four shuttles that attempted to flee the platform when [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen's]] squadron staged a diversionary attack on the outpost. Their aim was to give [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Zaletta]], an [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] who had grown sympathetic to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] cause, an opportunity to defect.

[[File:Aa23liftoff.jpg|thumb|left|300px|[[Kupalo]] makes his escape in ''AA-23''. Note the trussed-up custodian.]]

Onboard shuttle ''AA-23'', Zaletta fled the platform after the Rebels were able to disable the platform's shields. Unsure of which of the shuttles the defector was on, Azzameen was ordered to scan each craft before contact could be established. Their attack a success, the Rebels fled to a rendezvous with the Rebel [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B Frigate]] [[Redemption/Legends|''Redemption'']]. Zaletta safely docked with the vessel, but the group suddenly came under attack from an [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor]] and an escorting [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. With Zaletta under suspicion of luring the Rebels into a trap, he was imprisoned while the Rebels attacked the Interdictor and forced it to disengage.

Before the Rebels had a chance to decode the shuttle's valuable data, however, [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']]{{'s}} Tactical Officer [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Kupalo]], secretly a mole for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], blew his cover, breaking into the hangar. He bound the shuttle's custodian, placing a series of explosives beside him. Despite the deck officer's protests, Kupalo stole the shuttle and blasted his way out of the hangar, detonating the charges and causing huge damage to the hangar as he did so.

The ''Liberty'' rapidly deployed a trio of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] to pursue the shuttle, but Kupalo was prepared for this. From a hidden compartment on ''AA-23''{{'s}} underside, he fired three deadly [[Type-B Homing Mine]]s. Unable to evade the blindingly fast [[Warhead/Legends|warheads]], the X-wings were destroyed, and Kupalo made the jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The crew of the ''Liberty'' was able to track his destination, but suspected he would make multiple jumps to hinder pursuit, giving them very little time. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Yamarus]] dispatched pilots [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] and [[Olin Garn]] in a pair of [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-Wings]] to intercept the traitor.

[[File:Aa23mines.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''AA-23'' fires [[Type-B Homing Mine|mines]] from its underbelly.]]

Azzameen and Garn eventually caught up with Kupalo, who refused to stand down without a fight. While Garn fought off a flight of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]], Azzameen attacked ''AA-23''. Kupalo was forced to power the shuttle down after sustained heavy damage. [[Storm Unit (Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport)|''Storm Unit'']] then arrived to recover the shuttle and return to the ''Liberty'', while the two pilots fought reinforcements from "Kupalo's ride", the Star Destroyer [[Imperator (Imperial-class)|''Imperator'']]. ''AA-23'' was stripped of its databanks so that they could be decoded. Zaletta was cleared of all charges and presented his data to the Rebels, which would lead to the Alliance's discovery of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] at the planet [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Thanks to the overused tactic of capturing shuttles, each Lambda Shuttle was then fitted with a self destruct device if captured to prevent slip of information or data.

==Behind the scenes==
The use of "AA-23" is likely a reference to [[Detention Block AA-23/Legends|Detention Block AA-23]] where [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] was held aboard the first Death Star.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''
